summary:
For proven cases of medical negligence under Section 304A of the IPC, while no explicit duration exists, court practices generally imply a potential maximum term around three to five years based on analogous provisions and precedents indicating an escalation scale reflecting varying degrees of fault.
The maximum penalty typically ranges from about one year to over ten years depending upon severity; however, common practice suggests approximately three-to-five-year terms are more likely given existing guidelines and past rulings focusing heavily on intent behind actions leading to harm rather than mere oversight errors alone.
